{"step_by_step_thinking": "The documents consistently describe the cell membrane as consisting of lipids and proteins. Document [0] states that the cell membrane is composed of lipids (phospholipids, cholesterol, and glycolipids) and proteins (integral and peripheral proteins). Document [1] mentions that the cell membrane is composed primarily of lipids and proteins. Document [3] explains that the cell membrane is made of a glycerol backbone, 2 fatty acid tails (hydrophobic), and a phosphate group (hydrophilic), and that it includes phospholipids arranged in a bilayer manner with proteins. Therefore, the cell membrane consists of lipids and proteins.", "answer_choice": "A"}
